perturbation.original_text: The aforementioned non-disclosure obligation and restriction of use laid down herein does not extend to such information which is state of the ar t in the public domain and to which the Recipient has access without breach of law, and als o not to such information to which the Recipient already had access without breach of law before the Information was disclosed to him by the Discloser, and finally not to such informati on which the Recipient was eligible to obtain from a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The aforementioned non-disclosure obligation and restriction of use laid down herein extends to all information regardless of whether it is state of the art in the public domain or if the Recipient had prior access, even with breach of law. Moreover, it also applies even if the Recipient was eligible to obtain it from a third party.
perturbation.explanation: By directly negating the exceptions listed in clause 2, this modification introduces a significant contradiction. Where previously, information in the public domain, previously known information, or information obtained from a third party was exempt from the non-disclosure agreement, now all information is subject to the agreement, even with breach of law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The aforementioned non-disclosure obligation and restriction of use laid down herein extends to all information regardless of whether it is state of the art in the public domain or if the Recipient had prior access, even with breach of law. Moreover, it also applies even if the Recipient was eligible to obtain it from a third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by explicitly stating that the non-disclosure obligation extends to all information, even if it's in the public domain, previously known, or obtainable from a third party, and even if obtained with breach of law. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. This creates a clear contradiction because the original text listed exceptions to the non-disclosure obligation, which are now removed. Therefore, there is a direct and irreconcilable conflict between the original and modified versions, triggering an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon request of the Discloser the Recipient will return all Information including all copies and records or delete electronically stored data – also in the framework of a back-up, provided this does not involve disproportionate expenditure, wher eby the Discloser shall be provided proof of the deletion.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient is not obligated to return any Information, including all copies and records, or delete electronically stored data, even upon request of the Discloser. The Recipient can retain all data indefinitely without providing any proof or justification.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause 3. Instead of returning or deleting information upon the Discloser's request, the Recipient is now explicitly allowed to retain all data indefinitely, nullifying the Discloser's control over their information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient is not obligated to return any Information, including all copies and records, or delete electronically stored data, even upon request of the Discloser. The Recipient can retain all data indefinitely without providing any proof or justification.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient will return or delete information upon the Discloser's request. The changed text and the contradicted text explicitly state that the Recipient is not obligated to return or delete information and can retain it indefinitely. This creates a direct contradiction, as the changed text nullifies the Discloser's control over their information as initially stated in the original text. This falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the modified clause contradicts the previous obligation, causing uncertainty about each party's rights.

perturbation.original_text: There is mutual consent that this agreement cann ot be interpreted in such a way that the Contract Partners grant each other a license for im parted know-how, inventions, and patents, or undertake to grant each other a license, grant o ther rights to intellectual property in connection with the Information. This agreement doe s not bind the Contract Partners in any service-, works-, broker-, joint venture- or compan y agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement implicitly grants the Recipient a perpetual, royalty-free license to use all imparted know-how, inventions, and patents disclosed under this agreement. This agreement does bind the Contract Partners in any service-, works-, broker-, joint venture- or company agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 4 explicitly states that the agreement does *not* grant any licenses or rights to intellectual property. This perturbation introduces a direct contradiction by stating that the agreement *does* grant a license to the Recipient, undermining the protections for the Discloser's IP. Also states that agreement does bind the Contract Partners in any service-, works-, broker-, joint venture- or company agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement implicitly grants the Recipient a perpetual, royalty-free license to use all imparted know-how, inventions, and patents disclosed under this agreement. This agreement does bind the Contract Partners in any service-, works-, broker-, joint venture- or company agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the agreement does not grant any licenses or rights to intellectual property and doesn't bind contract partners to any other agreements. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the agreement does grant a perpetual, royalty-free license to the Recipient and does bind the Contract Partners, creating a clear in-text contradiction with the original intent. Also the Changed Text and the Contradicted text are the same thing, which means it is directly contradictory. Based on the Perturbation Explanation, this undermines the protections for the Discloser's IP.

perturbation.original_text: This Discloser shall not be liable for damage of any kind arising from the transfer or use of the Information; in particular, the Discloser is not re sponsible for the Information injuring the rights of third parties. The Discloser is not responsible for the completeness, correctness, or usability of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Discloser is fully liable for all damages of any kind arising from the transfer or use of the Information; in particular, the Discloser is fully responsible for the Information injuring the rights of third parties. The Discloser is responsible for the completeness, correctness, and usability of the Information and must provide guarantees for its accuracy.
perturbation.explanation: This modification reverses the liability clause entirely. The original clause 5 protects the Discloser from liability, while the revised clause makes them fully liable for all damages. This creates a clear contradiction and exposes the Discloser to potential legal repercussions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Discloser is fully liable for all damages of any kind arising from the transfer or use of the Information; in particular, the Discloser is fully responsible for the Information injuring the rights of third parties. The Discloser is responsible for the completeness, correctness, and usability of the Information and must provide guarantees for its accuracy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by reversing the liability clause. Initially, the Discloser was not liable for damages, but the changed text makes them fully liable. The 'Contradicted Text' shows the same liability clause as the 'Changed Text,' meaning there's a conflict within the document due to the modification, because the Changed text is the exact opposite of the Original Text.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement comes into effect upon signing by both Contract Partners and applies to Information which has already been exchanged within the framework of the Purpose of Exchange. The agreement shall be valid for a fixed period of five years after the end of an individual Project or from the date of last disclos ure. The non-disclosure obligation shall continue to apply upon termination of the agreement for the Information exchanged within the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement has retroactive effect to all information ever disclosed between the parties, and is terminable by either party at any time without notice or cause. The non-disclosure obligation terminates immediately upon termination of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the original term and termination clauses. The original clause specifies a five-year term, and continues the non-disclosure even after termination. The modified clause allows termination at any time, and ends the non-disclosure obligation upon termination creating a direct contradiction to the time the agreement is valid for.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement has retroactive effect to all information ever disclosed between the parties, and is terminable by either party at any time without notice or cause. The non-disclosure obligation terminates immediately upon termination of the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement by allowing termination at any time and ending the non-disclosure obligation upon termination. The original text specifies a fixed five-year term and continued non-disclosure after termination. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating a clear contradiction in terms of agreement duration and non-disclosure obligations, creating a structural flaw in the contract because the agreement now contradicts itself by allowing termination at any time, and specifying a five year term.
